name: FAERS (FDA Adverse Event Reporting System)
description: Post-market adverse event and medication error reports for drugs and biologics. Spontaneous reporting system where manufacturers, healthcare professionals, and patients submit reports of suspected adverse reactions. Subject to well-documented underreporting bias (the Weber effect). Quarterly data files available via openFDA API and bulk download.

name: MAUDE (Manufacturer and User Facility Device Experience)
description: Medical device adverse event reports from manufacturers, healthcare facilities, and voluntary reporters. Includes malfunctions, injuries, and deaths associated with medical devices. Monthly updates. Mandatory reporting for manufacturers and facilities; voluntary for patients and clinicians.

name: OpenFDA
description: RESTful API providing harmonized access across multiple FDA datasets including drug adverse events (FAERS), device events (MAUDE), drug labeling (SPL), food recalls, and NDC directory. Excellent for programmatic access with JSON responses and bulk download options.

name: 510(k) Premarket Notification Database
description: All 510(k) substantial equivalence clearances for medical devices since the Medical Device Amendments of 1976. Includes predicate device information, product codes, applicant details, and decision summaries. The primary pathway for moderate-risk device market entry.

name: PMA (Premarket Approval) Database
description: High-risk (Class III) medical device approvals and supplements. Includes original PMAs, panel track supplements, 180-day supplements, and real-time supplements. Contains approval dates, conditions, and links to summary of safety and effectiveness data.

name: NDC (National Drug Code) Directory
description: Unique identifiers for all finished commercial drug products including prescription, OTC, and unapproved drugs. Contains labeler codes, product codes, package codes, proprietary names, dosage forms, routes of administration, and active ingredients. Updated daily.

name: Orange Book
description: Lists all FDA-approved drugs with therapeutic equivalence ratings (A/B ratings). Critical reference for generic drug substitution decisions. Includes patent and exclusivity information. Used by pharmacists, insurers, and formulary committees nationwide.

name: GUDID (Global Unique Device Identification Database)
description: UDI data for medical devices including device identifiers, company information, device characteristics, and product codes. Supports the FDA UDI system mandated for device traceability. Hosted by NLM with API access and bulk downloads.

name: Sentinel System
description: Active post-market surveillance system using distributed queries across health insurers and integrated delivery systems. Largest such system worldwide with ~138.7M active members. Uses a common data model to query claims and EHR data without centralizing patient records. Public tools and routine query reports available.

name: DailyMed
description: Up-to-date FDA-approved drug labeling in Structured Product Labeling (SPL) format. Includes package inserts, warnings, contraindications, boxed warnings, dosing, and pharmacology. Searchable by drug name, NDC, class. API and bulk download available.

name: Medical Device Recall Database
description: Class I/II/III device recalls with root cause analysis, distribution information, and corrective actions. Includes voluntary and mandatory recalls. Searchable by product, firm, recall class, and date.

name: PubMed / MEDLINE
description: Biomedical literature citations and abstracts from MEDLINE, life science journals, and online books. Covers medicine, nursing, dentistry, veterinary medicine, healthcare systems, and preclinical sciences. MeSH-indexed with sophisticated search capabilities. Some records date to the 1800s.

name: ClinicalTrials.gov
description: Registry and results database for clinical studies conducted worldwide across 226 countries. Includes protocols, outcomes, enrollment data, sponsor information, and posted results. Mandatory registration for most FDA-regulated trials since 2007. Over 2M monthly visitors.

name: GenBank
description: Comprehensive annotated collection of all publicly available DNA sequences covering 581K+ species. Part of the International Nucleotide Sequence Database Collaboration (INSDC) with EMBL-EBI and DDBJ. Doubles roughly every 18 months. Bulk download and API access available.

name: dbGaP (Database of Genotypes and Phenotypes)
description: Archives genotype-phenotype interaction studies with both controlled-access and open-access tiers. Contains individual-level genotype data, phenotype variables, study protocols, and molecular data from genome-wide association studies.

name: GEO (Gene Expression Omnibus)
description: Functional genomics data repository for array-based and sequence-based expression data including microarray, RNA-seq, ChIP-seq, ATAC-seq, and methylation data. MIAME-compliant submissions with curated metadata. GEO2R tool for differential expression analysis.

name: dbSNP
description: Catalog of human genetic variation including single nucleotide polymorphisms, short insertions/deletions, and microsatellites. Each record (refSNP) aggregates submissions from multiple sources with population frequency data, clinical significance, and functional annotations.

name: RefSeq
description: Curated, non-redundant reference sequences including genomic DNA, transcripts, and proteins. Provides a stable reference for genome annotation, gene identification, and mutation analysis. Distinct from GenBank in being curated and non-redundant.

name: SRA (Sequence Read Archive)
description: Raw next-generation sequencing data repository storing unprocessed reads from Illumina, PacBio, Oxford Nanopore, and other platforms. Largest repository of raw sequencing data globally. Cloud-accessible via AWS and GCP.

name: PubChem
description: Chemical information resource providing structures, properties, biological activities, safety data, patents, and literature references. Absorbed former TOXNET content (HSDB, ChemIDplus, TRI). Includes 1.8M+ bioassays. Open REST API and bulk FTP.

name: SEER (Surveillance, Epidemiology, and End Results)
description: Population-based cancer incidence, survival, prevalence, and mortality data from registries covering ~48% of the US population. Gold-standard source for cancer statistics. Includes tumor site, stage, histology, first course of treatment, demographics, and follow-up for vital status.

name: TCGA (The Cancer Genome Atlas)
description: Multi-dimensional genomic characterization of major cancer types including whole-genome sequencing, RNA-seq, methylation, copy number, and proteomics. Landmark NIH project that fundamentally reshaped cancer biology. Data available via GDC portal.

name: GDC (Genomic Data Commons)
description: Unified data repository for NCI cancer genomics programs (TCGA, TARGET, CGCI, CPTAC, and others). Provides harmonized variant calls, gene expression, clinical data with standardized pipelines. Petabytes of genomic data with API and portal access.

name: BioLINCC
description: Repository of NHLBI-funded clinical trial data and epidemiologic study data including Framingham, ARIC, MESA, JHS, CARDIA, and others. Provides de-identified participant-level data for approved research. Covers cardiovascular, pulmonary, blood, and sleep disorders.

name: ImmPort
description: Shared clinical and molecular data from NIAID-funded immunology research. Contains flow cytometry, gene expression, clinical assessments, and adverse events data. Supports data reuse and meta-analysis across immunology studies.

name: IEDB (Immune Epitope Database)
description: Catalog of experimentally characterized immune epitopes including antibody and T cell epitopes. Curated from published literature with standardized assay descriptions. Includes analysis tools for epitope prediction and population coverage estimation.

name: All of Us Research Program
description: Longitudinal diverse biobank cohort with genomics, EHR data, surveys, physical measurements, and wearable device data. Emphasis on historically underrepresented populations. Researcher Workbench provides cloud-based analysis environment. One of the largest US precision medicine initiatives.

name: Medicare Provider Utilization and Payment Data
description: Provider-level utilization, charges, and Medicare payments by HCPCS procedure code for Medicare Part B. Includes individual physician-level billing data. Annual releases with provider NPIs, specialties, and geographic information.

name: MedPAR (Medicare Provider Analysis and Review)
description: Medicare Part A inpatient hospital and skilled nursing facility stay-level data. Each record represents a complete stay with diagnoses, procedures, charges, payments, length of stay, provider information, and patient demographics.

name: Chronic Conditions Data Warehouse (CCW)
description: Linked Medicare/Medicaid claims, enrollment data, assessments, and chronic condition flags. Supports research on chronic disease patterns, comorbidities, and healthcare utilization among Medicare and Medicaid beneficiaries. Summary statistics publicly available; individual-level data restricted.

name: Care Compare (Provider Data Catalog)
description: Quality ratings, patient experience scores, outcomes measures, and demographic information for Medicare-certified hospitals, physicians, nursing homes, home health agencies, dialysis facilities, and hospices. Star ratings and individual quality measures.

name: Medicare Cost Reports
description: Annual cost report data from Medicare-certified hospitals, SNFs, home health agencies, renal dialysis facilities, and other institutional providers. Contains detailed financial information including revenue, expenses, bed counts, and utilization.

name: Medicaid State Drug Utilization Data
description: Drug utilization data by state for Medicaid fee-for-service and managed care programs. Includes number of prescriptions, units reimbursed, and total Medicaid amount reimbursed by NDC and state. Quarterly releases.

name: NADAC (National Average Drug Acquisition Cost)
description: National average drug acquisition costs for covered outpatient drugs at the NDC level. Based on retail community pharmacy surveys. Updated weekly. Used as benchmark for Medicaid pharmacy reimbursement.

name: Medicare Part D Drug Spending
description: Total spending, utilization, and per-unit cost for Medicare Part D covered drugs. Includes brand and generic breakdowns, year-over-year cost changes, and beneficiary counts. Annual release.

name: Hospital Price Transparency Data
description: Hospital-specific standard charges for items and services. Machine-readable files required since January 2021 under the Hospital Price Transparency Rule. Includes gross charges, discounted cash prices, payer-specific negotiated charges, and de-identified minimum/maximum rates.

name: CDC WONDER
description: Public query system providing access to mortality (detailed and compressed), natality, population estimates, STDs, TB, environmental data, vaccine adverse events, and cancer statistics. Tabular and map-based output. The workhorse query tool for US public health researchers.

name: NVSS Mortality
description: Cause-of-death data from all death certificates filed in the US. Includes underlying and contributing causes coded to ICD-10, demographics, geographic detail, and manner of death. Microdata available via restricted-use files; aggregate via WONDER.

name: NVSS Natality
description: Birth certificate data including demographics, prenatal care, risk factors, pregnancy complications, labor and delivery characteristics, and newborn health. Complete count of US births. Public-use and restricted-use file variants.

name: NHANES (National Health and Nutrition Examination Survey)
description: Nationally representative survey combining interviews, physical examinations, and laboratory tests. Covers chronic diseases, nutritional status, environmental exposures, infectious diseases, body composition, dental health, and biomarkers. Continuous since 1999 with 2-year cycles.

name: BRFSS (Behavioral Risk Factor Surveillance System)
description: Largest continuously conducted health survey in the world. State-based telephone survey on health-related risk behaviors, chronic conditions, and use of preventive services. Covers all 50 states, DC, and territories. Provides state-level and some county/metro estimates.

name: NHIS (National Health Interview Survey)
description: Annual cross-sectional household interview survey on health status, insurance coverage, healthcare access and utilization, health behaviors, and chronic conditions. One of the oldest health surveys in the US. Redesigned in 2019.

name: NNDSS (National Notifiable Diseases Surveillance System)
description: Weekly and annual reports of nationally notifiable infectious and non-infectious conditions reported by state and territorial health departments. Covers ~120 conditions including TB, STIs, hepatitis, vaccine-preventable diseases, and emerging infections.

name: WISQARS (Web-based Injury Statistics)
description: Interactive online system providing fatal and nonfatal injury, violent death, and cost of injury data. Draws from NVSS mortality, NEISS-AIP nonfatal injury surveys, and BRFSS. Generates custom reports by mechanism, intent, demographics, and geography.

name: YRBSS (Youth Risk Behavior Surveillance System)
description: Biennial school-based survey of high school students measuring health-risk behaviors including alcohol/drug use, sexual behavior, dietary behaviors, physical activity, and violence. National, state, and local surveys.

name: USCS (US Cancer Statistics)
description: Combined CDC NPCR and NCI SEER cancer incidence and mortality data from population-based registries. Covers 97%+ of the US population. Official federal statistics for cancer incidence by site, stage, demographics, and geography.

name: National Health Care Surveys (NHCS family)
description: Family of surveys covering ambulatory care (NAMCS), hospital ambulatory care (NHAMCS), nursing homes, hospice, and home health. Provides national estimates of healthcare service utilization patterns, diagnoses, procedures, and treatments across care settings.

name: VAERS (Vaccine Adverse Event Reporting System)
description: Spontaneous post-licensure vaccine safety surveillance system. Accepts reports from healthcare providers, patients, and manufacturers. Subject to reporting bias and cannot determine causality. Primarily a signal detection tool.

name: HCUP NIS (National Inpatient Sample)
description: Largest publicly available all-payer US inpatient hospital discharge database. 20% stratified sample of all community hospitals. Contains diagnoses, procedures, charges, length of stay, patient demographics, and hospital characteristics. Used extensively in health services research.

name: HCUP NEDS (Nationwide Emergency Department Sample)
description: All-payer emergency department visit database including both treat-and-release visits and those leading to admission. Contains diagnoses, procedures, charges, expected payment source, and disposition. Complements the NIS.

name: HCUP KID (Kids' Inpatient Database)
description: Nationwide all-payer inpatient database for children (age 0-20). Produced every 3 years. Contains diagnoses, procedures, charges, and demographics for pediatric hospitalizations. Only national pediatric inpatient database.

name: MEPS (Medical Expenditure Panel Survey)
description: Nationally representative survey of healthcare utilization, expenditures, insurance coverage, and payment sources for the US civilian non-institutionalized population. Longitudinal 2.5-year panel design. Links household, medical provider, and employer components.

name: CAHPS (Consumer Assessment of Healthcare Providers)
description: Standardized patient experience surveys covering health plans, hospitals, clinician groups, home health, hospice, and emergency departments. Used for public reporting, quality improvement, and value-based purchasing. HCAHPS hospital survey is mandatory for CMS.

name: NSDUH (National Survey on Drug Use and Health)
description: Annual nationally representative survey measuring substance use (alcohol, tobacco, illicit drugs, non-medical prescription drugs), mental illness prevalence, and treatment utilization among the US civilian population aged 12+. Public-use microdata available.

name: TEDS (Treatment Episode Data Set)
description: Treatment admissions (TEDS-A) and discharges (TEDS-D) from state-funded substance abuse treatment facilities. Includes substance types, demographics, referral source, prior treatment episodes, and employment status. Record-level public-use files available.

name: N-SUMHSS (Substance Use and Mental Health Services Survey)
description: Annual census of all known substance abuse and mental health treatment facilities in the US. Includes services offered, payment methods accepted, client counts, OTP status, and special programs. Merged N-SSATS and NMHSS in 2021.

name: AHRF (Area Health Resources Files)
description: County-level data on health professions, facilities, population demographics, economics, education, and hospital utilization. Over 6,000 variables compiled from 50+ data sources. Essential reference for health workforce and access analysis.

name: UDS (Uniform Data System)
description: Annual performance data from federally qualified health centers including demographics, services, clinical quality measures, financial information, and staffing. Covers ~30M patients served at community health centers.

name: NPPES (NPI Registry)
description: National Provider Identifier registry containing all HIPAA-covered healthcare providers with taxonomy codes, practice addresses, organizational affiliation, and provider type. Bulk download and API access available.

name: VA Open Data
description: Aggregate and summary data from the VA healthcare system. Full EHR-derived clinical data (CDW) is restricted. Public datasets include wait times, facility locations, satisfaction surveys, and research datasets. VA operates the largest integrated healthcare system in the US.

name: VASQIP (VA Surgical Quality Improvement Program)
description: Risk-adjusted surgical outcomes data from VA hospitals. Pioneered evidence-based surgical quality measurement. Tracks 30-day morbidity and mortality outcomes. Basis for the civilian ACS-NSQIP program.

name: CEBS (Chemical Effects in Biological Systems)
description: Toxicology study data from National Toxicology Program bioassays including 2-year carcinogenicity studies, genetic toxicity tests, reproductive/developmental toxicity studies, and modified one-generation studies. Searchable by chemical, study type, and endpoint.

name: UMLS (Unified Medical Language System)
description: Metathesaurus linking biomedical terminology systems including ICD, SNOMED CT, MeSH, LOINC, RxNorm, CPT, and 200+ others. Essential interoperability resource for mapping between coding systems. Includes Semantic Network and SPECIALIST Lexicon.

name: RxNorm
description: Normalized naming system for generic and branded drugs providing standard names and codes for clinical drugs. Maps between NDC, drug knowledge bases, pharmacy systems, and EHRs. Critical interoperability standard for e-prescribing and clinical decision support.

name: Health IT Dashboard
description: EHR adoption rates, health information exchange metrics, interoperability measures, and patient access statistics. National and state-level dashboards tracking health IT adoption and use across hospitals and physician offices.

name: HealthData.gov
description: Federal open data portal aggregating health datasets from CMS, CDC, FDA, AHRQ, SAMHSA, HRSA, and other HHS agencies. Includes machine-readable datasets with metadata, APIs, and visualization tools.

name: WHO Global Health Observatory (GHO)
description: Health statistics covering mortality, burden of disease, SDGs, NCDs, risk factors, health systems, and environmental health for all 194 WHO member states. Biweekly updates. API and bulk download access.

name: WHO Mortality Database
description: Cause-of-death data by age, sex, and country using ICD codes. The most comprehensive collection of mortality data worldwide. Quality varies significantly by country and time period.

name: ICTRP (International Clinical Trials Registry Platform)
description: Meta-registry aggregating ClinicalTrials.gov and 17+ other national clinical trial registries worldwide. Provides a unified search interface for global clinical trial registration. Essential for detecting duplicate trials and research gaps.

name: VigiBase
description: World's largest database of individual case safety reports (ICSRs) for suspected adverse drug reactions. Contributed by 180+ national pharmacovigilance centers. Public access via VigiAccess; full data for qualified users.

name: EudraVigilance
description: Suspected adverse drug reactions for medicines authorized in the European Economic Area. Reports from healthcare professionals, patients, and marketing authorization holders. Public access dashboard with line listings.

name: EU Clinical Trials Register
description: Protocol and results information for clinical trials conducted in the EU/EEA. Being superseded by the Clinical Trials Information System (CTIS) under the new EU Clinical Trials Regulation.

name: ECDC Surveillance Atlas
description: Surveillance case data for notifiable infectious diseases across EU/EEA member states. Interactive atlas with temporal trends, geographic distribution, and demographic breakdowns via the TESSy surveillance system.

name: OECD Health Statistics
description: Comprehensive health statistics across OECD member countries covering expenditure, resources, utilization, quality indicators, pharmaceutical market, long-term care, and health status. Key source for cross-country health system comparisons.

name: World Bank Health Indicators
description: Health expenditure as % GDP, life expectancy, immunization rates, mortality, nutrition, and disease prevalence for 200+ countries. Part of the World Development Indicators collection. API access available.

name: GBD (Global Burden of Disease)
description: Most comprehensive epidemiological effort to date. Estimates mortality, morbidity, DALYs, YLLs, YLDs for 292 causes of death, 375 diseases/injuries, and 88 risk factors across 204 countries and 660 subnational locations. Published annually in The Lancet.

name: DHS (Demographic and Health Surveys)
description: Population, health, HIV, nutrition survey microdata from low- and middle-income countries. Household-level surveys with standardized methodology enabling cross-country comparisons. Gold standard for LMIC health data.

name: HES (Hospital Episode Statistics)
description: Admissions, outpatient episodes, and A&E attendances in NHS hospitals in England. Contains diagnoses (ICD-10), procedures (OPCS), demographics, and geographic identifiers. One of the largest single-payer hospital datasets globally.

name: CPRD (Clinical Practice Research Datalink)
description: UK primary care electronic health records covering ~20% of the population. Linked to hospital (HES), mortality, cancer registry, and socioeconomic data. One of the largest longitudinal primary care databases globally. Widely used in pharmacoepidemiology.

name: Health Canada Drug Product Database
description: Human, veterinary, and disinfectant drug products approved for sale in Canada. Includes drug identification numbers, active ingredients, routes, dosage forms, and company information.

name: Australia DAEN (Database of Adverse Event Notifications)
description: Adverse event reports for medicines and medical devices in Australia. Searchable by product name, active ingredient, or medical term. Includes severity and outcome information.

name: GWAS Catalog
description: Curated catalog of genome-wide association studies and their SNP-trait associations. Includes 85K+ summary statistic datasets. Manual curation from published literature with standardized ontology terms. Essential resource for genetic epidemiology.

name: ArrayExpress / BioStudies
description: Functional genomics experiment archive for microarray and sequencing data. Substantial mirror/overlap with GEO. Being integrated into the BioStudies platform. MIAME-compliant metadata.

name: Ensembl
description: Genome browser with gene models, variants, regulation, and comparative genomics for 300+ species. Provides standardized genome annotations, BioMart data mining, REST API, and programmatic access. Updated roughly every 2 months.

name: UniProt
description: Comprehensive protein sequence and functional annotation resource. Swiss-Prot (reviewed, curated) and TrEMBL (unreviewed, automatic) components. Includes protein function, domains, modifications, interactions, disease associations, and 3D structures.

name: PDB (Protein Data Bank)
description: Atomic-level 3D structures of biological macromolecules determined by X-ray crystallography, NMR, and cryo-EM. Plus 1M+ AlphaFold computed structure models. Operational since 1971, making it one of the oldest open-access scientific databases.

name: DrugBank
description: Comprehensive drug data including chemical structures, targets, mechanisms, interactions, pharmacokinetics, and pharmacodynamics. Covers FDA-approved small molecules, biologics, investigational compounds, and nutraceuticals. Free academic access; commercial license required.

name: ChEMBL
description: Curated database of bioactivity measurements linking chemical compounds to biological targets. Data extracted from medicinal chemistry literature by expert curators. Includes binding affinities, functional assays, and ADMET properties.

name: PharmGKB
description: Pharmacogenomics knowledge base linking drugs to genes, variants, and clinical outcomes. Includes clinical annotations, dosing guidelines, drug label annotations, and variant-drug pathway information. CPIC clinical guidelines hosted here.

name: OMIM (Online Mendelian Inheritance in Man)
description: Comprehensive catalog of human genes and genetic disorders with literature-backed annotations. Authoritative source for Mendelian disease-gene relationships. Each entry is a detailed review article with full references. Founded by Victor McKusick in 1966.

name: Orphanet
description: Inventory, classification, and encyclopedia of rare diseases. Includes orphan drug information, expert centers, diagnostic labs, patient registries, and clinical trial listings. European reference for rare disease information. Multilingual.

name: COSMIC (Catalogue of Somatic Mutations in Cancer)
description: Somatic mutation data for human cancers curated from published literature and large-scale genomic screens. Includes point mutations, structural variants, gene fusions, and copy number data. 223 expert-curated cancer gene census entries.

name: ICGC (International Cancer Genome Consortium)
description: International consortium for comprehensive whole-genome analysis of cancers. PCAWG project analyzed 2,658 whole genomes across 38 cancer types. Largest coordinated cancer whole-genome sequencing project to date.

name: cBioPortal
description: Exploration and visualization portal for multidimensional cancer genomics datasets from TCGA, ICGC, MSK-IMPACT, and other sources. Web-based tools for querying mutations, copy number, expression, and clinical data across hundreds of studies.

name: UK Biobank
description: Deep phenotyping + genotyping cohort of 500K UK volunteers aged 40-69 at recruitment. Includes genomics, imaging (brain/cardiac/abdominal MRI, DXA), biomarkers, questionnaires, accelerometry, and linked NHS health records. One of the most impactful biomedical resources of the 21st century.

name: MIMIC-IV
description: De-identified electronic health records from a major US academic medical center. Contains vitals, labs, medications, nursing notes, radiology reports, procedures, and diagnoses. Most widely used open-access clinical dataset for health AI research.

name: Framingham Heart Study
description: Landmark longitudinal cardiovascular cohort study spanning 3 generations since 1948. Identified major cardiovascular risk factors (cholesterol, blood pressure, smoking, diabetes). Now includes genomic data. One of the most influential epidemiologic studies in history.

name: Cochrane Database of Systematic Reviews
description: Gold-standard systematic reviews and meta-analyses of healthcare interventions. Rigorous methodology with protocol registration, comprehensive search, risk of bias assessment, and GRADE certainty ratings. The premier evidence synthesis resource.

name: GHDx (Global Health Data Exchange)
description: Meta-catalog of health-related datasets worldwide. Directory of surveys, censuses, vital registration systems, and other health data sources. Gateway to GBD results and underlying data. Essential for finding what data exists in specific countries/topics.

name: HTA Database
description: Health technology assessment reports from agencies worldwide. Evaluations of clinical effectiveness, cost-effectiveness, and organizational impact of health technologies. Key resource for evidence-based policy.

name: Reactome
description: Curated peer-reviewed biological pathways and reactions database. Covers signal transduction, metabolism, gene regulation, immune system, cell cycle, and disease pathways. Visualization and analysis tools for pathway enrichment.

name: AJRR (American Joint Replacement Registry)
description: Hip and knee arthroplasty outcomes registry tracking implant performance, revision rates, patient-reported outcomes, and surgical complications. Public annual reports; raw data restricted to participants.

name: STS National Database
description: Cardiac, thoracic, and congenital heart surgery outcomes. Tracks mortality, morbidity, complications, and patient satisfaction. Public aggregate benchmarks and star ratings; participant-level data restricted.

name: IRIS (Integrated Risk Information System)
description: Human health assessments for environmental chemicals. Provides reference doses (RfD), reference concentrations (RfC), and cancer classifications. Gold-standard risk values used by federal and state agencies for regulatory decisions.

name: TRI (Toxics Release Inventory)
description: Annual releases and transfers of toxic chemicals from industrial facilities. Covers 770+ chemicals from ~21K facilities. Includes air emissions, water discharges, land disposal, and off-site transfers. Publicly searchable and downloadable.

name: EJScreen
description: Environmental justice screening tool combining demographic indicators with environmental burden metrics (pollution, proximity to hazards). Census-tract level for the entire US. Identifies communities with disproportionate environmental health risks.

name: ATSDR Toxicological Profiles
description: Comprehensive toxicological profiles for hazardous substances found at Superfund sites. Each profile reviews health effects, toxicokinetics, biomarkers, and exposure data. Developed through systematic literature review and peer review.

name: DAWN (Drug Abuse Warning Network)
description: Drug-related emergency department visits tracking substances involved, patient demographics, and disposition. Originally discontinued in 2011; being revived by CDC. Historical data covering 1994-2011 available.

name: PRAMS (Pregnancy Risk Assessment Monitoring System)
description: State-specific population-based survey of women who recently had a live birth. Covers prenatal care, insurance, substance use, breastfeeding, contraception, intimate partner violence, and postpartum depression across ~47 participating states.

name: NSCH (National Survey of Children's Health)
description: National and state-level prevalence estimates of children's health conditions, access to care, family wellbeing, adverse childhood experiences (ACEs), and special healthcare needs. Annual since 2016.

name: Linked Birth-Infant Death Files
description: Birth certificate data linked to death certificate for infant deaths. Enables analysis of birthweight-specific mortality rates, maternal risk factors, and infant death causes. Used for Perinatal Periods of Risk analysis.

name: NIS (National Immunization Survey)
description: Vaccination coverage estimates by vaccine type, demographics, and geography. Provider-verified for children (NIS-Child). Includes teen and adult components. Gold-standard source for US immunization coverage rates.

name: VSD (Vaccine Safety Datalink)
description: Near-real-time vaccine safety monitoring via linked EHR/claims data from 9 integrated health systems. Active surveillance population for detecting adverse events following vaccination. Public aggregate summaries; individual data restricted.

name: SVI (Social Vulnerability Index)
description: Composite index of 16 census variables capturing socioeconomic status, household characteristics, minority status, and housing/transport vulnerability. Census-tract level for the entire US. Widely used in disaster preparedness and health equity research.

name: County Health Rankings
description: County-level health outcomes and health factors including clinical care, health behaviors, social/economic factors, and physical environment. Composite rankings encourage county-level health improvement. Annual release with media attention.

name: PLACES (CDC)
description: Small-area estimates for chronic disease risk factors, health outcomes, and prevention measures at census-tract level. Uses BRFSS data plus model-based estimation to produce local estimates. Successor to the 500 Cities project.

name: TCIA (The Cancer Imaging Archive)
description: De-identified cancer medical images (CT, MRI, PET, digital pathology) with clinical annotations. Over 150 collections spanning multiple cancer types. Freely downloadable for research. Primary resource for medical imaging AI development.

name: OpenNeuro
description: Open repository for neuroimaging data in BIDS format including MRI, fMRI, EEG, MEG, and iEEG. Community-driven with standardized metadata. Supports reproducible neuroscience research.

name: ADNI (Alzheimer's Disease Neuroimaging Initiative)
description: Longitudinal neuroimaging, biomarker, clinical, and genetic data tracking progression from normal aging through MCI to Alzheimer's disease. MRI, PET (amyloid, tau), CSF biomarkers, cognitive testing, and genetics.

name: NIH Chest X-ray / CheXpert
description: Frontal chest radiographs with NLP-extracted or radiologist-labeled pathology labels for 14 conditions. Widely used benchmark for chest X-ray AI models. CheXpert (Stanford) adds uncertainty labels and expert annotations.

name: UK Biobank Imaging
description: Population-scale multi-modal imaging (brain MRI, cardiac MRI, abdominal MRI, DXA body composition) linked to genetic, lifestyle, and health outcome data. Largest imaging genetics resource globally.

name: GTEx (Genotype-Tissue Expression)
description: Gene expression and eQTL data linking genetic variation to tissue-specific gene regulation across 54 human tissue types from ~1,000 post-mortem donors. Essential resource for interpreting GWAS results and understanding tissue-specific gene regulation.

name: eICU Collaborative Research Database
description: Multi-center ICU dataset from Philips eICU telehealth program. Contains vitals, labs, diagnoses, treatment plans, APACHE scores, and nurse charting from 208 US hospitals. Complements the single-center MIMIC dataset.

name: Synthea
description: Open-source synthetic patient generator producing realistic FHIR/C-CDA records. Simulates complete patient lifespans with encounters, conditions, medications, procedures, and observations based on epidemiological models. Widely used for testing and education.

name: CMS Synthetic Public Use Files
description: Synthetic Medicare claims data mimicking real distributions for inpatient, outpatient, carrier, DME, and Part D claims. Designed for testing, training, and software development without privacy concerns. Based on 2008-2010 patterns.

name: N3C (National COVID Cohort Collaborative)
description: Harmonized EHR data for COVID-19 research from 75+ contributing sites using OMOP common data model. Contains diagnoses, labs, medications, vitals, and outcomes. Largest US COVID research dataset. Cloud-based enclave access.

name: NAACCR (Cancer Registry Standards)
description: Standardized cancer incidence data covering ~97% of the US population aggregated from state cancer registries. Public aggregate statistics via Cancer in North America (CiNA). Sets standards for cancer data collection and reporting.

name: CFFPR (Cystic Fibrosis Patient Registry)
description: Clinical outcomes for CF patients at accredited care centers including lung function, infections, nutrition, transplant, and mortality. Annual report publicly available. One of the most successful disease-specific registries.

name: OPTN (Organ Procurement and Transplantation Network)
description: Organ transplant data covering waitlist, donors, transplant events, graft survival, and patient survival. Public aggregate data; research files via SRTR. Mandated by the National Organ Transplant Act.

name: USRDS (United States Renal Data System)
description: National registry of end-stage renal disease with incidence, prevalence, treatment modality (dialysis vs transplant), survival, costs, and comorbidities. Annual data report publicly available. Research files for approved investigators.

name: NCDB (National Cancer Database)
description: Hospital-based cancer registry from 1,500+ CoC-accredited hospitals covering ~72% of newly diagnosed US cancers. Contains staging, treatment, and outcomes. Public benchmark reports; research file restricted to CoC-accredited programs.

name: NCDR (National Cardiovascular Data Registry)
description: Multiple sub-registries tracking cardiac catheterization/PCI (CathPCI), ICD implants, chest pain protocols, EP ablation, and other cardiovascular procedures. Aggregate benchmarks public; participant-level data restricted.

name: USDA FoodData Central
description: Food composition data including nutrients, portions, and branded products. Integrates Foundation Foods (analytical), SR Legacy (standard reference), FNDDS (survey), and Branded Foods (industry data). API and bulk download.

name: FDA Total Diet Study
description: Ongoing surveillance measuring levels of nutrients and chemical contaminants in the typical US diet as consumed (i.e., prepared/cooked foods). Market baskets collected from retail outlets nationwide. Longest-running FDA food safety program.

name: OSHA Injury and Illness Data
description: Establishment-level injury and illness rates from OSHA 300A annual summary logs. Includes total case incidence rate, DART rate, and case counts by industry and establishment size.

name: OSHA Fatality Investigations
description: Narratives and citations from workplace fatality and catastrophe investigations. Includes event descriptions, contributing factors, and OSHA citations issued. Searchable by industry, event type, and date.

name: BLS SOII (Survey of Occupational Injuries and Illnesses)
description: National estimates of nonfatal workplace injuries and illnesses. Incidence rates by industry, nature of injury, body part, event/exposure, and days away from work. Complementary to OSHA data with broader coverage.

name: CFOI (Census of Fatal Occupational Injuries)
description: Complete census of all fatal work injuries in the US verified via multiple source documents (death certificates, workers comp, OSHA, medical examiner). Includes event, source, occupation, industry, and demographics.

name: Dartmouth Atlas of Health Care
description: Geographic variation in Medicare utilization, spending, surgical rates, and end-of-life care. Mapped to Hospital Referral Regions and Health Service Areas. Pioneered the study of unwarranted practice variation in US healthcare.

name: AHA Annual Survey Database
description: Hospital-level data on bed counts, admissions, FTEs, payer mix, service offerings, and organizational structure. The most comprehensive hospital-level database in the US. Public summary tables; full data is licensed/paid.

name: WHO Essential Medicines List
description: Model list of essential medicines that should be available in national health systems. Updated biennially by expert committee. Guides national formulary decisions worldwide. Includes core list and complementary list.

name: EU Union Register of Medicinal Products
description: Centrally authorized human and veterinary medicinal products in the EU. Includes EPARs (European Public Assessment Reports) with scientific assessment details. Only covers centrally authorized products, not nationally approved ones.

name: Japan PMDA Drug Information
description: Approved drugs, medical devices, and safety information for Japan. Includes review reports, package inserts, and adverse drug reaction case reports. English interface available for some content.

name: NIOSH Worker Health Chartbook
description: Occupational health indicators derived from BLS, NVSS, BRFSS, and SOII data. Covers nonfatal/fatal injury rates, respiratory conditions, hearing loss, and blood lead levels by industry and occupation.

name: MedlinePlus
description: Consumer health information resource with articles on health conditions, drugs, supplements, and medical tests. Linked to NIH content, clinical trials, and medical dictionaries. Available in English and Spanish. 400M+ annual page views.

name: IIS (Immunization Information Systems)
description: Consolidated state-level patient immunization records. Federated system across 64 US jurisdictions. Public data availability varies by state. Used for coverage assessment, vaccine reminders, and supply management.

name: BSA Medicare Claims PUFs
description: Non-identifiable claim-level Medicare data for public download. Includes inpatient, outpatient, carrier, and prescription drug claims. Small samples useful for education and methodology development.

name: HRSA Data Warehouse
description: Maps, dashboards, and data on HRSA grants, loan/scholarship programs, Health Professional Shortage Areas (HPSAs), Medically Underserved Areas (MUAs), and organ transplant data. Central HRSA data portal.
